verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, the major inducible cytoplasmic chaperone. Under baseline conditions it is predominantly cytosolic/nuclear. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in multiple non-baseline states: (1) heat shock and other cellular stresses induce surface HSP70 display on tumor cells; (2) cancer cells constitutively display ecto-HSP70 on their outer leaflet, which has been exploited by NK-cell-based and antibody-based immunotherapy programs; (3) immunogenic cell death and apoptosis also expose HSP70 on the PM outer face. These are cell-state-induced (stress/oncogenic transformation/ICD) surface exposures qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced.
